questions.q: What was the capital city of King Drupada's Panchala?
questions.explanation: Drupada ruled from Kampilya, the southern Panchala capital where Draupadi's swayamvara was later held.

questions.q: Who ruled Panchala at the time of the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: King Drupada, Drona's boyhood friend turned bitter enemy, ruled Panchala and fathered its fire-born children.

questions.q: Which queen of the epic was born in Panchala and named for it?
questions.explanation: Draupadi rose from her father's sacrificial fire in Panchala, and the land gave her the famous name Panchali.

questions.q: Which fire-born prince of Panchala commanded the Pandava armies?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna, born armed and armored from Drupada's sacrifice, led the Pandava host for all eighteen days.

questions.q: Which famous contest was held in Panchala?
options: The dice game for the kingdom
Draupadi's swayamvara of the fish's eye
The tournament of the Kuru princes
questions.explanation: At Kampilya, suitors had to string the great bow and strike a target raised high on a revolving device — pictured as a fish in popular tradition — to win Draupadi's hand.

questions.q: Which neighboring power was Panchala's ancient rival?
questions.explanation: Panchala and the Kuru kingdom were old rivals and uneasy neighbors, a tension that shaped the whole epic.

questions.q: Who seized half of Panchala as revenge for an old insult?
questions.explanation: Drona had his Kuru pupils defeat and capture Drupada, then kept half the kingdom as the price of the king's old contempt.

questions.q: What does Draupadi's name Panchali mean?
questions.explanation: Panchali simply marks her as the princess of Panchala, just as Gandhari is named for Gandhara.

questions.q: From what were Drupada's children Dhrishtadyumna and Draupadi born?
options: A boon of Shiva spoken aloud
questions.explanation: Both emerged full-grown from Drupada's great sacrifice — Dhrishtadyumna armed and armored from the flames, Draupadi dark and dazzling from its altar.

questions.q: Why did Drona bear a grudge against King Drupada?
options: Drupada had stolen Drona's herd of cattle
Drupada had mocked the poor brahmin's claim to their boyhood friendship
Drupada had banished Drona from Panchala
Drupada had refused Drona's son a bride
questions.explanation: When the impoverished Drona came seeking help, King Drupada sneered that friendship exists only between equals.

questions.q: Who captured Drupada in battle to pay Drona's teaching fee?
questions.explanation: After the Kaurava princes failed, Arjuna stormed Kampilya with his brothers and dragged Drupada in bonds before Drona.

questions.q: How was Panchala divided after Drona's victory over Drupada?
options: Drona kept the lands north of the Ganga and returned the south to Drupada
Drona took the capital and left Drupada the countryside
The kingdom was split between Drupada's two sons
Drona gave the whole realm to Ashwatthama and kept nothing
questions.explanation: Drona retained northern Panchala for himself and handed back the southern half, declaring they were now equals and friends.

questions.q: Which sages conducted the sacrifice through which Drupada obtained his fire-born children?
questions.explanation: Drupada persuaded the brahmin brothers Yaja and Upayaja to perform the rite that yielded Dhrishtadyumna and Draupadi.

questions.q: For what explicit purpose did Drupada commission his great sacrifice?
options: To win back the northern half of his kingdom
To obtain a son destined to kill Drona
To secure a place for himself in heaven
To bring rain after a seven-year drought
questions.explanation: Burning for revenge, Drupada commissioned the rite to obtain a son who would slay Drona; the same sacrifice also yielded his daughter Draupadi.

questions.q: Which child of Drupada was the princess Amba reborn to destroy Bhishma?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi, born a girl and raised as a son in Panchala, carried Amba's vengeance to Bhishma on the tenth day.

questions.q: Where did the Pandavas lodge in Kampilya when they came for the swayamvara?
options: In a temple of Shiva outside the walls
In the house of a potter
In the camp of the visiting kings
questions.explanation: Still disguised as brahmins, the brothers and Kunti took shelter in a potter's house in Drupada's capital.

questions.q: By which other clan names are the Panchala warriors known throughout the epic?
questions.explanation: The epic frequently calls Drupada's people Somakas and Srinjayas, after illustrious ancestors of the Panchala line.

questions.q: What bond had once existed between Drupada and Drona?
options: They were cousins through their mothers
They had studied together as boys in the same hermitage
They had served the same king as ministers
They had been rivals for the same bride
questions.explanation: As boys they studied side by side in the hermitage of Drona's father Bharadwaja, where Drupada promised to share his fortune.

questions.q: What test did suitors face at the swayamvara held in Panchala?
options: To string a colossal bow and strike the eye of a fish revolving high overhead
To bend a bow of iron across the knee and snap it
To shoot an arrow through seven palm trees
To lift the great bow of Shiva above the head
questions.explanation: Kings failed even to string the bow, until Arjuna in brahmin disguise struck the revolving target overhead and won Draupadi; the fish is the traditional image of the mark, and aiming at its reflection in oil belongs to later retellings, not Vyasa's text.

questions.q: By what other name was King Drupada known?
questions.explanation: Drupada was also called Yajnasena, which is why Draupadi bears the name Yajnaseni.

questions.q: Who secretly followed the disguised victors of the swayamvara and concluded they must be kshatriyas?
options: The priest of the royal house
Dhrishtadyumna, hidden at the potter's house
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna shadowed the brahmin youths home, listened to their talk of war and weapons, and reported that they were warriors.

questions.q: Which two Panchala princes guarded the wheels of Arjuna's chariot at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: The brothers Yudhamanyu and Uttamaujas protected Arjuna's chariot wheels throughout the war and died in the night raid.

questions.q: Which city became the capital of the northern half of Panchala that Drona took?
questions.explanation: Drona's northern share was ruled from Ahichchhatra, while Drupada kept the south with Kampilya on the Ganga.

questions.q: Who killed King Drupada in the Kurukshetra war?
options: Drona, shortly before his own death
questions.explanation: Drona cut down his old enemy Drupada, along with King Virata, on the fifteenth day, shortly before Dhrishtadyumna beheaded him.

questions.q: Who annihilated the surviving Panchala leadership in the night raid after the war's last day?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama butchered Dhrishtadyumna, Shikhandi, and the sleeping Panchalas while Kripa and Kritavarma held the camp gates.

questions.q: Who was Drupada's father, giving him the patronymic Parshata?
questions.explanation: Drupada was the son of King Prishata, and the name Parshata for his line passes on to Dhrishtadyumna as well.

questions.q: Who finally persuaded a doubtful Drupada to accept Draupadi's marriage to all five Pandavas?
options: Vyasa, by revealing her past life and divine design
Kunti, by citing her unbreakable word
Krishna, by threat of Yadava arms
questions.explanation: Vyasa told Drupada of Draupadi's former life, in which she won a five-fold boon of husbands, and the king consented.

questions.q: With which modern Indian state is Drupada's capital Kampilya usually identified?
options: Uttar Pradesh, at Kampil in Farrukhabad district
questions.explanation: Kampilya is generally identified with the town of Kampil on the Ganga in Farrukhabad district, Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: What command did old Drupada himself hold in the Pandava host?
options: Keeper of the camp and supplies
Leader of one of the seven akshauhinis
questions.explanation: Drupada was named among the seven leaders of the Pandavas' seven akshauhinis, alongside Virata, Dhrishtadyumna, and others.

questions.q: Despite the prophecy of his own death, what did Drona do for the fire-born prince of Panchala?
options: He refused to ever meet him
He demanded the boy's thumb as fee
He bound him by oath never to fight the Kurus
He accepted Dhrishtadyumna as a pupil and taught him the use of arms
questions.explanation: Knowing the boy was born to kill him, Drona magnanimously trained Dhrishtadyumna in weapons, trusting fate to take its course.
